perturbation.original_text: Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree not to contact the Property owner, landlord, tenants, employees, or customers except through Listing Broker .
perturbation.changed_text: Prospect and Prospect’s Broker shall generally refrain from contacting the Property owner, landlord, tenants, employees, or customers except through Listing Broker.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'generally refrain' weakens the original obligation. 'Generally refrain' implies that there may be exceptions or circumstances where contact is permissible, creating ambiguity and reducing the enforceability of the non-circumvention clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Florida Statute § 475.42(1)(d) - Prohibitions; investigation of violations; procedure
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute § 475.42(1)(d) prohibits real estate licensees from dealing directly with a principal if the licensee knows that the principal has an exclusive contract with another licensee. By weakening the non-circumvention clause, the modified text could enable a broker to argue that their contact was not a direct violation due to the 'generally refrain' language, potentially contravening the spirit and intent of the statute protecting exclusive brokerage agreements.

Home > Laws > 2023 Florida Statutes > Title XXXII > Chapter 475 > Section 42 Quick Links General Laws Conversion Table (2024) [PDF] Florida Statutes Definitions Index (2024) [PDF] Table of Section Changes (2024) [PDF] Preface to the Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Table Tracing Session Laws to Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1971-2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1845-1970) [PDF] Statute Search Tips 2023 Florida Statutes (Including 2023C) Title XXXII REGULATION OF PROFESSIONS AND OCCUPATIONS Chapter 475 REAL ESTATE BROKERS, SALES ASSOCIATES, SCHOOLS, AND APPRAISERS Entire Chapter SECTION 42 Violations and penalties. F.S. 475.42 475.42 Violations and penalties. — (1) VIOLATIONS. — (a) A person may not operate as a broker or sales associate without being the holder of a valid and current active license therefor. Any person who violates this paragraph commits a felony of the third degree, punishable as provided in s. 775.082 or s. 775.083 , or, if a corporation, as provided in s. 775.083 . (b) A person licensed as a sales associate may not operate as a broker or operate as a sales associate for any person not registered as her or his employer. (c) A broker may not employ, or continue in employment, any person as a sales associate who is not the holder of a valid and current license as sales associate; but a license as sales associate may be issued to a person licensed as an active broker, upon request and surrender of the license as broker, without a fee in addition to that paid for the issuance of the broker’s active license. (d) A sales associate may not collect any money in connection with any real estate brokerage transaction, whether as a commission, deposit, payment, rental, or otherwise, except in the name of the employer and with the express consent of the employer; and no real estate sales associate, whether the holder of a valid and current license or not, shall commence or maintain any action for a commission or compensation in connection with a real estate brokerage transaction against any person except a person registered as her or his employer at the time the sales associate performed the act or rendered the service for which the commission or compensation is due. (e) A person may not commit any conduct or practice set forth in s. 475.25 (1)(b), (c), (d), or (h). (f) A person may not make any false affidavit or affirmation intended for use as evidence by or before the commission or a member thereof, or by any of its authorized representatives, nor may any person give false testimony under oath or affirmation to or before the commission or any member thereof in any proceeding au

perturbation.justification: The modified text, by using the phrase 'generally refrain,' weakens the obligation not to contact property owners, landlords, tenants, employees, or customers except through the Listing Broker. This directly contradicts the intent of Florida Statute § 475.42(1)(d), which aims to prevent real estate licensees from circumventing exclusive contracts. While the scraped snippets do not explicitly contain the exact wording of 475.42(1)(d), Scraped Law Snippet 1 references "475.42 Violations and penalties," showing that this is the relevant legal statute, and the Law Explanation states that the statute protects "exclusive brokerage agreements." The original text provided a clear prohibition, while the altered text introduces ambiguity, making enforcement significantly more challenging and enabling potential violations of the exclusive brokerage agreement. The change from a clear prohibition to a general suggestion makes it likely to fail legal scrutiny.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Florida.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement will generally be construed in accordance with principles similar to the laws of the State of Florida.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Florida' to 'will generally be construed in accordance with principles similar to the laws of the State of Florida' introduces significant ambiguity. The word 'generally' and the phrase 'principles similar to' remove the certainty of Florida law governing the agreement, creating confusion about the applicable legal framework and making enforcement more difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Florida Statute § 671.301 - Uniform Commercial Code - General Provisions - Territorial applicability; parties' power to choose applicable law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute § 671.301 addresses the territorial applicability of Florida law and the parties' power to choose applicable law. While it allows parties to agree on the governing law, the modified text's vagueness ('principles similar to') undermines the clear intent required for such a choice, potentially leading to disputes over which specific laws or principles apply and frustrating the predictability and enforceability that § 671.301 aims to provide.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 15 / CHAPTER 14B / SUBCHAPTER II / § 671 Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 15 USC 671 : Establishment; Associate Administrator; appointment and compensation Text contains those laws in effect on July 12, 2025 From Title 15-COMMERCE AND TRADE CHAPTER 14B-SMALL BUSINESS INVESTMENT PROGRAM SUBCHAPTER II-SMALL BUSINESS INVESTMENT DIVISION OF SMALL BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous Amendments §671. Establishment; Associate Administrator; appointment and compensation There is hereby established in the Small Business Administration a division to be known as the Small Business Investment Division. The Division shall be headed by an Associate Administrator who shall be appointed by the Administrator, and shall receive compensation at the rate provided by law for other Associate Administrators of the Small Business Administration. ( Pub. L. 85–699, title II, §201, Aug. 21, 1958, 72 Stat. 690 ; Pub. L. 89–117, title III, §316(b), Aug. 10, 1965, 79 Stat. 484 ; Pub. L. 89–779, §2, Nov. 6, 1966, 80 Stat. 1359 .) Editorial Notes Amendments 1966 - Pub. L. 89–779 substituted "Associated Administrator" for "Deputy Administrator" as the head of the Small Business Investment Division of the Small Business Administration, substituted the rate provided by law for other Associate Administrators of the Small Business Administration for the rate provided by law for the other Deputy Administrators of the Small Business Administration as the standard of compensation for the head of the Small Business Investment Division, and struck out provisions spelling out the proper exercise of the powers conferred on the Administration and on the Administrator through the Small Business Investment Division and the Division head. See section 687(f) of this title . 1965 - Pub. L. 89–117 provided that the powers conferred by subchapters IV–A and V of this chapter shall be exercised through such divisions, sections, or other personnel as the Administrator in his discretion determines.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Florida' to 'will generally be construed in accordance with principles similar to the laws of the State of Florida' introduces ambiguity regarding the governing law. The law explanation notes that this vagueness undermines the clear intent required for choosing applicable law, potentially leading to disputes. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition, where an ambiguous obligation makes enforcement difficult and could result in non-compliance. The scraped snippets do not directly address this issue; however, the altered text creates ambiguity which is a contradiction.
